subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #32 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Gardening with Ms. Linda
Suddenly everything is in bloom, and it feels like summer. This week we spread topsoil and compost throughout the garden. The children took turns loading the wheelbarrow and making what they refer to as “deliveries”. We discussed why it’s important to amend (improve) the soil each year. The plants we grow, especially ‘heavy feeders’, like tomatoes, lettuce and cucumbers require a lot of nutrients to produce their flowers, fruit and leaves. Compost adds humus that helps soil retain moisture, suppress pests and diseases. We worked this week to prepare the soil so we can plant cool weather crops next week.
PEP Earth Day Yard Sale
April 19th - But ...
Please check email!
Given the pending rain, please check your email this weekend for status of the yard sale and the potential to move to the rain date, Sunday April 26th.
Mark your calendars for our Earth Day Yard Sale, happening right here on the SOCDS campus from 11:00 AM – 3:00 PM. Entry is a suggested donation of $15, with 100% of proceeds going to support PEP and Ms. Linda's amazing Garden Project. Additional contributions are always gratefully welcomed and please feel free to invite friends and family.
Volunteers Needed! We're looking for helping hands for setup (9–11 AM) and breakdown (3–4 PM). If you're able to lend a hand, please reach out to Mary Payne at
marypawlina@gmail.com
or (302) 864-8344.
Pop Blossom Studio Playdate –
Few Spots Still Available!
A few spots remain in the morning and afternoon sessions of our bird/spring-themed art playdate with Pop Blossom Studio! Join us on Sunday, May 17th for a creative, sensory experience designed to spark imagination and build creative confidence — plus children will take home a special project. The fee is $40 per child, with $5 going directly to SOCDS PEP.
Sign up here:
Morning (10:30am - 11:30am)

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
We had our warm weather streak this week, but it is going to be more seasonal the coming week.  Again, solid shoes and layers are a good approach for children to be comfortable for 30 minutes on the playground.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please 
send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If 
you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
